title: Eriocaulon compactum
intro: Eriocaulon compactum, also known as Indian pipeplant, is a perennial aquatic plant species native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Eriocaulon compactum include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eriocaulon compactum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and alleviate digestive problems.
taxonomy: Eriocaulon compactum belongs to the family Eriocaulaceae and is classified as a species of the genus Eriocaulon. This plant is a type of sedge, and its classification is within the order Poales. The classification of Eriocaulon compactum is further categorized under the subfamily Eriocauloideae. The family Eriocaulaceae is a group of plants that are commonly known as pipeworts. Eriocaulon compactum's classification is also recognized within the phylum Angiospermophyta.
